title: fix: find_first_paragraph leaks code-fence content into handoff snippet
description: find_first_paragraph() in prime.rs does not track code-fence state. When handoff sections are empty (HTML comment placeholders only), it falls through to the Context section and picks up git-status lines from inside a triple-backtick block. Reproducer: wai prime in REPLy.jl shows RESUMING with 'M  .beads/issues.jsonl' instead of 'no summary yet'. Fix: toggle in_code_block on backtick-fence lines and skip their content.

title: Wire TCP transport for tracer-bullet acceptance path
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 10 and tracer-bullet scope item "Target: eval over TCP" from `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md`.

Implement the TCP listener/server lifecycle so a real socket client can send a newline-delimited `eval` request and receive the expected value+done stream.

## Files
- `src/transport/tcp.jl`
- `src/server.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`
- `test/helpers/server.jl`
- `test/e2e/eval_test.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/e2e/eval_test.jl` passes for the definitive acceptance test: send `{"op":"eval","id":"1","code":"1+1"}` over TCP and receive `value` then `done`.
- [ ] The server can bind with `port=0` and exposes the assigned port to tests.
- [ ] Shutdown happens reliably in `finally` paths so tests do not leak sockets/tasks.
- [ ] `just test` passes for unit + integration + the main e2e acceptance path.

## Workflow Mandate
- Begin with the failing end-to-end acceptance test.
- Keep the transport limited to TCP; Unix sockets and other listeners remain deferred.
- Avoid speculative abstraction beyond what the tracer-bullet path exercises.

title: Harden tracer bullet for concurrent clients and disconnects
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 11-12 and the e2e hardening scenarios in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

Extend the tracer bullet to survive two concurrent clients and client disconnects mid-eval without violating protocol invariants or leaking server resources.

## Files
- `src/transport/tcp.jl`
- `src/server.jl`
- `src/session/`
- `test/e2e/eval_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/server.jl`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/e2e/eval_test.jl` covers two concurrent connections and client disconnect mid-eval.
- [ ] The server continues serving other clients after one client disconnects unexpectedly.
- [ ] No resource leaks or double-done responses appear in the new e2e scenarios.
- [ ] `just test` passes for the full tracer-bullet suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Add the failing e2e hardening tests before changing production code.
- Keep the work focused on robustness of the existing thin slice; do not expand scope to limits, security, or new operations.
- If cleanup falls out of the fix, keep it isolated or move it to the tidy ticket.

title: Implement eval middleware streaming for tracer bullet
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 5 and eval/error streaming coverage for `REQ-RPL-011`, `REQ-RPL-011b`, and the stdout/stderr ordering constraints described in the tracer-bullet design.

Implement the eval middleware that executes code in a session module, captures stdout/stderr, emits value messages, and distinguishes stderr chunks from error responses.

## Files
- `src/middleware/eval.jl`
- `src/eval/runtime.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/eval_middleware_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/eval_middleware_test.jl` covers stdout capture, stderr capture, value repr, empty code no-op behavior, streaming order, and err-field disambiguation.
- [ ] Production code evaluates against the provided session module and emits a done-terminated response stream.
- [ ] Runtime errors are left shape-compatible with the shared error response contract.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new eval middleware unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Start with failing eval middleware tests.
- Keep success-path green work separate from broader refactors.
- Do not add deferred features such as timeout enforcement, stdin, or silent/store-history handling.

title: Implement minimal middleware chain and error responders
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 6-7 plus coverage for `REQ-RPL-050`, `REQ-RPL-061`, and `REQ-RPL-063`.

Implement minimal middleware composition plus unknown-op and error response behavior so the tracer bullet can route `eval`, pass through unhandled ops, and emit protocol-conformant failures.

## Files
- `src/middleware/core.jl`
- `src/middleware/session.jl`
- `src/middleware/unknown_op.jl`
- `src/errors.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/middleware_test.jl`
- `test/unit/error_test.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/middleware_test.jl` covers pass-through for unhandled ops and interception for handled ops.
- [ ] `test/unit/error_test.jl` covers eval runtime error shape, parse error shape, unknown operation shape, status flags, and no-double-done behavior.
- [ ] Production code provides the minimal middleware chain: `SessionMiddleware -> EvalMiddleware -> UnknownOpMiddleware`.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new middleware and error unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Write failing middleware/error tests first.
- Keep the middleware subset intentionally minimal; deferred middlewares stay out of scope.
- Separate cleanup/generalization from the green implementation step.

title: Assemble tracer-bullet pipeline and pass integration tests
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 8-9 and the integration-layer expectations in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

Assemble the handler context and middleware pipeline so request Dicts can flow through the tracer-bullet stack without network transport.

## Files
- `src/pipeline.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl` passes for success, stdout ordering, unknown op, eval error, parse error, and ephemeral-session leak detection.
- [ ] Production code exposes a minimal pipeline assembly API used by higher-level transport code.
- [ ] Shared conformance assertions run cleanly against integration responses.
- [ ] `just test` passes for unit + integration layers.

## Workflow Mandate
- Start from the existing failing integration tests and only add implementation needed to satisfy them.
- Reuse the unit-tested components rather than re-encoding logic in the pipeline layer.
- Hold refactors for the tidy ticket unless they are strictly required to complete the assembly.

title: Implement message framing and protocol boundary checks
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 3 plus protocol coverage for `REQ-RPL-001`, `REQ-RPL-001b`, `REQ-RPL-004`, `REQ-RPL-005`, `REQ-RPL-007`, `REQ-RPL-008`, and `REQ-RPL-040b`.

Implement newline-delimited JSON parsing/serialization and boundary checks so requests/responses obey the tracer-bullet wire contract.

## Files
- `src/protocol/message.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/message_test.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/message_test.jl` covers parse/serialize, newline framing, malformed JSON, blank lines, partial reads, non-object JSON, disconnect handling, and overlong id rejection.
- [ ] Production code parses only flat JSON objects and serializes newline-terminated responses.
- [ ] Response helpers preserve request id echo and kebab-case keys needed by conformance checks.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new message-focused unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Write the failing message/protocol tests first.
- Make the smallest implementation that turns the targeted unit tests green.
- Avoid bundling unrelated session or transport behavior into this step.

title: Implement ephemeral session manager for tracer bullet
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 4 and session coverage for `REQ-RPL-030` and `REQ-RPL-035`.

Implement ephemeral light-session support backed by anonymous `Module`s so eval requests without a session can run in an isolated context and clean up afterward.

## Files
- `src/session/manager.jl`
- `src/session/module_session.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `test/unit/session_test.jl`
- `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl`

## Done
- [ ] `test/unit/session_test.jl` covers anonymous module creation, binding isolation, cleanup, and `session_count` leak detection.
- [ ] Production code exposes a session manager API sufficient for ephemeral session creation and teardown.
- [ ] The implementation supports the integration test expectation that session count returns to zero after an ephemeral eval completes.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new session-focused unit tests included in the suite.

## Workflow Mandate
- Drive the design from failing session tests first.
- Keep the implementation limited to ephemeral sessions; persistent/clone/close flows are out of scope.
- Defer structural cleanup or extra abstractions to the tidy ticket unless they are required to go green.

title: Scaffold tracer-bullet test harness and shared helpers
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression steps 1-2 and the shared helper design in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

Scaffold the acceptance/integration test targets and shared helper files before core implementation so the tracer bullet has a concrete red test shape.

## Files
- `test/runtests.jl`
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`
- `test/helpers/server.jl`
- `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl`
- `test/e2e/eval_test.jl`
- `src/REPLy.jl`

## Expected Helper APIs
- `test/helpers/conformance.jl`: `assert_conformance(msgs::Vector{Dict}, request_id::String)`
- `test/helpers/tcp_client.jl`: `collect_until_done(sock)::Vector{Dict}` plus the minimal request-send helper needed by e2e tests
- `test/helpers/server.jl`: `with_server(f; port=0)`

## Done
- [ ] `test/runtests.jl` includes helpers → unit → integration → e2e in the planned order.
- [ ] Shared helper files exist with minimal public APIs/stubs aligned to the design doc.
- [ ] `test/integration/pipeline_test.jl` and `test/e2e/eval_test.jl` contain the initial failing tracer-bullet cases.
- [ ] The suite fails for missing implementation reasons, not missing include/file errors.

## Workflow Mandate
- Start red: write failing tests/helpers before adding production code.
- Keep scaffolding minimal; do not implement transport/pipeline behavior here.
- Any cleanup beyond minimal setup belongs in the later tidy ticket.

title: Implement tracer bullet: eval over TCP thin slice
description:
## Context
Implement the tracer bullet defined in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/research/2026-04-17-tracer-bullet-scope-a-tracer-bullet-cuts-throu.md` and the outside-in TDD sequence in `.wai/projects/tracer-bullet/designs/2026-04-17-testing-validation-strategy-principle-o.md`.

This epic tracks the thinnest end-to-end slice: `eval` over TCP with newline-delimited JSON, ephemeral sessions, the minimal middleware chain, and done-terminated streaming responses.

## Files
- `src/REPLy.jl`
- `src/protocol/`
- `src/session/`
- `src/middleware/`
- `src/transport/`
- `test/helpers/`
- `test/unit/`
- `test/integration/`
- `test/e2e/`
- `test/runtests.jl`

## Done
- [ ] The tracer-bullet child issues are completed in dependency order.
- [ ] `just test` passes with the new end-to-end, integration, and unit coverage.
- [ ] The thin slice satisfies the target TCP eval exchange and shared protocol conformance checks.

## Workflow Mandate
- Follow outside-in TDD: acceptance tests first, then integration, then unit-driven implementation.
- Apply Tidy First: keep cleanup/refactor work separate from feature-green steps.
- Prefer small, reviewable commits and keep issue notes updated with design decisions.

title: Clean up GitHub Actions and run tests in pre-push hook
description:
Why: streamline CI automation and ensure local pushes are gated by the Julia test suite.

What needs to be done:
- review existing GitHub Actions workflows and simplify where appropriate
- update local hook configuration so pre-push runs the test suite
- adjust docs or task runner commands if needed
- validate the updated workflows and hooks

title: Tidy tracer-bullet implementation after green suite
description:
## Context
Plan section: TDD progression step 13.

Perform the post-green cleanup pass once the tracer-bullet tests are passing. This is the dedicated Tidy First follow-up for small structural improvements that became obvious during implementation.

Before starting, record the intended tidy goals in issue notes so this stays a bounded cleanup pass rather than an open-ended refactor bucket.

## Files
- `src/`
- `test/`
- `README.md`
- `justfile`

## Done
- [ ] Cleanup/refactor changes are behavior-preserving and backed by the passing tracer-bullet suite.
- [ ] Naming/module boundaries are improved only for the specific friction points identified during implementation.
- [ ] `README.md` is updated only if tracer-bullet developer usage or workflow changed.
- [ ] `justfile` is updated only if a stable new command or workflow is required by the tracer bullet.
- [ ] `just test` passes after the tidy pass.

## Workflow Mandate
- Do not mix this issue with the feature-green path; start only after the tracer-bullet suite is green.
- Keep refactors small, explicit, and behavior-preserving.
- Record noteworthy design lessons in issue notes and/or `wai` artifacts.

title: Scaffold minimal Julia package for REPLy.jl
description: Create a minimal Julia package scaffold for REPLy.jl with , , and  so repository automation has a real package to exercise. Keep it intentionally small and compatible with the existing just/test/coverage setup.

title: Ignore local external specification artifact
description: Add a gitignore rule for the untracked local file  so it does not keep appearing as workspace noise. Do not delete user data; just ignore it locally.
